mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances following a >0.5pp unemployment rise.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reveals supply-side bottlenecks and impending economic slowdown, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines >2% fail to precede negative 5-day SPX returns in late-cycle regimes (win rate < 45%).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly increases >0.4% are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms broad-based labor market strengthening, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Concurrent payroll growth and unemployment declines do not yield positive 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals successful supply chain normalization and rising manufacturing output, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases >1.5% in recovery regimes fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns (accuracy < 52%).
